If your life had a thesis statement, what would it be right now? What do you wish it were?
When was the last time you lost track of time because you were completely absorbed in something? What does that tell you about your purpose?
Write about a moment when you felt your life had deep significance. What made it meaningful?
If you could only be remembered for one thing, what would you choose—and why that over everything else?
What are you building that will outlast you? Does that matter to you? Why or why not?
List your five core values. Now look at last week's calendar. How many hours served those values?
Frankl wrote that meaning can be found in work, love, or suffering. Which source of meaning dominates your life?
What would you do differently if you knew your efforts would succeed? What would you do differently if they wouldn't?
Write a letter from your 90-year-old self to your current self. What does that person want you to know about what matters?
Is there a difference between what you say matters to you and how you actually spend your time? Explore the gap.
If finding your life purpose were less about discovery and more about creation, what would you build?
What activities make you lose track of time so completely that you forget to eat?
If you had a year with no financial obligations, how would you spend your first Monday morning?
What did you love doing at age 10 that you've abandoned? Why did you stop?
What topics can you talk about for hours without getting bored, even if your audience has glazed over?
What activities energize you even when they're physically or mentally exhausting?
What injustice or problem in the world makes you genuinely angry, not just sad?
If you could fix one thing about your local community by tomorrow, what would it be?
What group of people do you understand deeply because you've been one of them?
What conversations do you have where people say, 'I never thought of it that way'?
What pain have you experienced that, once healed, could help others going through the same thing?
What knowledge or perspective do you have that most people around you lack?
If you could volunteer full-time for any cause, what would it be and why?
What societal problem do you think about even when you're not directly affected by it?
Write about a time when you witnessed suffering and thought, 'Someone should do something about this.' What stopped you from being that someone?
What problems are people willing to pay you to solve?
What do your friends or colleagues ask for your help with most often?
If you could charge $100/hour for any skill you have, what would it be?
What do you do that people compliment you on repeatedly?
What value do you provide that AI and automation cannot replicate?
Write about a time you were paid well for work that felt meaningful. What made it meaningful?
How do you stay true to yourself in difficult situations?
What makes you unique, and how do you embrace it?
What are your greatest strengths, and how do you use them?
What makes you feel most alive?
What legacy do you want your goals to leave behind?
How do you define your dream life?
What does unconditional love look like to you?
Write about someone who has significantly influenced your life. What have you learned from them?
What parts of your past would you change if you could, and why?
Think about a fear you've overcome. How did you manage to do it?
What personal qualities do you think others admire in you?
What's a question you wish someone would ask you about your life, and how would you answer it?
When do you feel the most authentic, and what are you typically doing during those times?
If you woke up tomorrow with no fear, what would you do first?
If you had to describe yourself using only five adjectives, what would they be and why?
Write about a conflict that revealed a side of you that you didn't know existed.
If I could talk to my younger self, I would tell them...
What are three core beliefs you hold about yourself that might need reevaluation?
If you could slow time, what moment would you choose to savor?
What is a small detail in your environment that you had never noticed before? Why do you think you missed it?
What part of your day do you rush through the most, and what might you be missing?
When did you last feel completely calm? What helped you achieve that state?
In this moment, I am feeling...
What are three things you are grateful for today, and why?
Describe a time when you faced a significant challenge. How did you get through it?
List five questions you ask yourself before making a big decision.
If you could change one past decision, what would it be and why?
What are the values that you most want to live by?
When making decisions, I consider...
If you could make a decision that would significantly impact your life in five years, what would it be and why?
List the pros and cons of a recent major decision you made. Would you change anything about your choice now?
What are the obstacles that could prevent you from reaching your goals, and how do you plan to overcome them?
If all jobs paid the same, what profession would you choose and why?
If you had unlimited resources, what would be your biggest goal?
List the steps you are currently taking to reach one of your major life goals.
How do your goals impact the people around you? Do they involve or affect others in any significant way?
What does success look like to you in different areas of your life?
If you achieved all your current goals, what would your daily life look like?
In five years, I hope to...
What are three goals you hope to achieve within the next decade, and why are they important to you?
What is the most challenging part of your day, and what habit could you develop to make it easier?
What is a daily habit that has a positive impact on your life?
A pattern I've noticed in my life is...
Write a scene where the protagonist must make a tough decision that mirrors one you are currently facing.
If you can switch lives with anyone for a day, who would it be and why?
What steps can you take today to move closer to your career goals?
What is your dream job, and why do you want it?
List three professional skills you want to develop and why.
In my career, I want to achieve...
What was the most stressful experience of your life?
What's the most impactful 'no' you've said recently?
What's the best and worst piece of advice you've ever received?
Why did you decide to do what you are doing now in your life?
If you didn't have to sleep, what would you do with the extra time?
What question would you most like to know the answer to?
What could you give a 40-minute presentation on with absolutely no preparation?
What are some things you've had to unlearn?
What chapters would you separate your autobiography into?
What are you most likely very wrong about?
What was the most bizarre encounter you've had in your life?
What's the milestone you're working towards right now in your personal and professional life?
If a crystal ball could tell you the truth about yourself, your life, the future or anything else, what would you want to know?
If you could invite anyone, living or dead to dinner, who would that be and why?
What is something you love now, that you never could have imagined you would like in the past?
What one thing you would do if it would be impossible to fail?
What was a place or event that transformed your ideas, thinking, perspective, or made you come alive in a new way?
What would you like to change about your family?
Is there something you've dreamt of doing for a long time? Why haven't you done it?
What do you regret not doing? Why haven't you done it?
What would you like to ask yourself?
If you knew that in one year you would die suddenly, would you change anything about the way you are now living?
What's one thing that could happen today that would make it great?
What was a major turning point in your life?
What is the most challenging part of your job? How do you handle it?
What do you consider as your biggest achievement in the last 5 years?
When people come to you for help, what do they usually want help with?
When do you feel truly alive?
What's something you love about yourself?
What would constitute a perfect day for you?
What do you want your epitaph to be?
Did you ever feel lost in your life path? How did you find your way again?
What are you looking forward to in the coming months?
What small gesture from a stranger made a big impact on you?
What advice would you offer to yourself five years ago? One year ago?
What is something you know you do differently than most people?
What makes you feel most alive?
What do you spend too much time doing? What don't you spend enough time doing?
If you were to die this evening with no opportunity to communicate with anyone, what would you most regret not having told someone? Why haven't you told them yet?
What stands between you and happiness, and how do you overcome it?
Is there something you've dreamt of doing for a long time? Why haven't you done it?
Why did you decide to do what you're doing now with your life?
What would you regret not fully doing, being or having in your life?

What do you love most about yourself?
What is a memory that sticks with you till now, and why?
What is my number one goal for this week?
What are some things I have been putting off doing?
What bad habits should I stop doing?

What have I outgrown or am ready to release, even if it once felt familiar or safe?
When do I feel most connected to life? What activities, people, or places bring me home to myself?
What am I craving more of in the second half of the year—and what’s one small step I can take toward that?

What am I proud of—even if no one else sees it?
What do I want to remember when I feel stuck, scared, or unsure in the months ahead?

What activities make me lose track of time because I enjoy them so much?
What struggles have I overcome that I feel called to help others with?
If money wasn't a factor, how would I choose to spend my time each day?
What are people naturally drawn to me for? What advice or help do they often seek from me?
